methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $6M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Limelight Steel is a pre-seed stage climate and energy technology company founded by Olivia Dippo that has raised $6M to date. The broader Climate & Energy sector includes 8 private peers and 1 public comparable (Linz AG, LNZA), though direct peer valuation data for pre-seed stage companies in this space remains constrained—the 19 same-sector peers identified lack sufficient narrow-stage filtering to support a reliable peer-median approach.

The valuation analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of pre-seed to next-stage advancement. Using the $6M total raised as a value proxy, this method implies a $15M post-money valuation. Given the single-method basis and inherent uncertainty in pre-seed stage visibility, a triangulated range of $13M to $17M captures reasonable bounds around this median estimate, reflecting ±15% sensitivity to multiplier assumption and comparable stage transitions.

The primary limitation is methodological: peer-median valuation cannot be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with published data. The analysis would strengthen materially upon identification of additional pre-seed or seed-stage climate technology valuations, or upon clarity regarding Limelight's specific technology focus within the broad climate and energy sector. Founder track record and burn rate remain outside this quantitative scope but merit investor due diligence.
